propositions: Enable verify.completion_floor_active=true in this project's rebar.toml to dogfood the completion floor on live reviews while the schema default stays False for clients.

The completion floor is fully implemented, wired into review_plan, and calibrated (gold-set 30/30 floor decisions correct, κ=1.0, zero must-never-suppress anchors dropped) but has never been switched on.

Document the monitoring surface so wrongly-suppressed findings are recoverable: suppressions persist to the plan-review sidecar dropped[] tagged drop_reason="completion" plus coverage.completion_floored_finding_ids.
Optionally add a per-drop logger.info at the drop site so live suppressions are visible without opening the sidecar, since the successful-drop path currently logs nothing.

This is a config-activation and monitoring change only with no logic change; a wrong suppression is advisory-only and cannot change a PASS/BLOCK verdict, with rollback by setting the flag back to false.
A live container plan-review of epic 8d27 ran the floor with the flag on, floored zero findings (none qualified, fail-safe no-op), and left the verdict PASS unchanged.
